items.summary: Audit and fix the full persistence pipeline so that every admin change made through the admin panel is durably saved to the Motoko backend canister via the useCMSBackend hook and survives page reloads and return visits. Specifically: (1) Verify that every CMSContext mutation action (addArtwork, updateArtwork, deleteArtwork, updateSiteSettings, updateNavigationItems, updateFAQItems, updateProcessSteps, updateCareerMilestones, addTestimonial, updateTestimonial, deleteTestimonial, addPressMention, updatePressMention, deletePressMention, addBlogPost, updateBlogPost, deleteBlogPost, addNotification, markNotificationRead, markNotificationResponded, deleteNotification, and all others) correctly calls its corresponding backend save function from useCMSBackend after updating local React state. (2) Verify that CMSContext correctly loads data from the backend on mount (via loadAllCMSData) and hydrates all state slices from the backend response instead of falling back to in-memory defaults when backend data is non-empty. (3) Verify that the Motoko backend actor in backend/main.mo declares all CMS data stable variables correctly so they survive canister upgrades and redeployments. (4) Fix any broken wiring, missing await/async calls, type conversion errors, or silent failures in useCMSBackend.ts that would cause backend writes to silently fail. After this fix, an admin who edits artwork details, site settings, testimonials, process steps, or any other CMS content must see those changes still in place after navigating away and returning to the site.
